retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, MHC Class I -- Related Testing -- Sequence-specific primer polymerase chain reaction
retrieved_docs.content: Sequence-specific primer polymerase chain reaction (PCR) uses various primers complementary to specific HLA DNA sequences. The DNA is plated into a multiwell plate with different primers. If the DNA extracted from that cell is complementary to the primer, it is amplified, and the product can be run on a gel via electrophoresis. The band can be identified as a primer and matched to known candidate HLA alleles. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Molecular Genetics Testing -- Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: In manual Sanger sequencing, the user reads all 4 gel lanes simultaneously, moving from bottom to top to identify the terminal ddNTP for each band. For instance, if the bottom band is found in the ddGTP column, then the smallest PCR fragment terminates with ddGTP, and the first nucleotide from the 5’ end of the original sequence has a guanine (G) base. [42] Automated Sanger sequencing employs a computer to read each capillary gel band sequentially, using fluorescence to determine the terminal ddNTP identity. Laser activation of fluorescent tags emits light, detected by the computer, with each ddNTP tagged with a unique fluorescent label. The output is a chromatogram displaying fluorescent peaks corresponding to each nucleotide along the template DNA's length. [43]

retrieved_docs.title: Biochemistry, Protein Synthesis -- Fundamentals
retrieved_docs.content: Transfer RNA (tRNA): tRNAs are adaptors bridging the nucleotide sequence found in mRNAs to the amino acid sequence found in a growing protein. Transfer RNAs assume a cloverleaf-like secondary structure with an amino acid linked to its 3’ end through an ester linkage and a stretch of three nucleotides at the base of the cloverleaf referred to as the anticodon. The three bases of the anticodon base pair with complementary codon sequences in an mRNA during the process of protein synthesis.  This base-pairing interaction plays a critical role in the readout of the genetic code from mRNA to protein. There are 20 different aminoacyl-tRNA synthetases, one for each of the 20 common amino acids. Once an amino acid links to its cognate tRNA it is referred to as an aminoacyl tRNA, or “charged” tRNA. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, MHC Class I -- Related Testing -- Sequence-specific oligonucleotide probes
retrieved_docs.content: One way to detect the high polymorphism seen in these genes is via PCR paired with sequence-specific oligonucleotides. This method involves amplifying the gene via PCR and then probing DNA with a fluorescent tag. The HLA type is determined using known HLA alleles as a reference, and that gene may undergo sequencing. [8] [9] [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, MHC Class I -- Related Testing -- Direct DNA sequencing
retrieved_docs.content: Another technique is to use Sanger sequencing or next-gen sequencing to sequence the entire gene of a specific HLA variant after amplification via PCR. Once the sequence is known, it can be compared to previously published HLA alleles. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: Genetics, Human Major Histocompatibility Complex (MHC) -- Testing
retrieved_docs.content: 3- Sequence-based typing (SBT): This technique is performable on several different platforms. One is Sanger-based DNA sequencing, and the other platforms have their basis in next-generation sequencing. The most common application of SBT typing is to identify  allelic level HLA typing for stem cell transplantation recipients and donors. An allele refers to a unique nucleotide sequence for a gene. By DNA based typing nomenclature, allelic resolution utilizes all the digits in a current allele name. (A*01:01:01:01). This methodology allows for confirmation of new allelic sequences. [2] [13] [14]

retrieved_docs.content: ERDITZE ZAILA BIZIRIK JAIOTAKO HAURRA (K) is linked to the obstetric technique described as bevelling; forceps, laag, and this connection is indicated by a mapped_from relationship, showing that the association has been derived or aligned from an external source.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric practice, the overarching topic “Indications for care in pregnancy; labor; and delivery” includes the specific condition “Malposition; malpresentation,” which is classified as a subcategory (has parent) within these care indications. This relationship highlights that malposition and malpresentation are considered part of the broader set of indications guiding clinical management during pregnancy, labor, and delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2431
retrieved_docs.content: Cervical shortening in pregnancy is categorized as a component of other complications of birth and puerperium that influence the management of the mother, and it specifically encompasses the sub‑condition of cervical shortening occurring in the second trimester.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 15 -- Level 3 -- Community 2387
retrieved_docs.content: Other procedures inducing or assisting delivery is a broad obstetric category that encompasses the specific outcome of فشل التوليد بالملقط (forceps delivery failure). Within this framework, الولادة بالملقط (forceps delivery) is directly linked to فشل التوليد بالملقط, indicating that the failure represents a particular adverse event or subcategory of the forceps delivery procedure. Consequently, both الولادة بالملقط and its associated failure, فشل التوليد بالملقط, are situated under the larger grouping of other procedures that induce or assist delivery, highlighting their interrelated roles in assisted vaginal birth and the importance of recognizing potential complications.
